methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Epoch Biodesign is a Series A biotech company founded by Jacob Nathan that has raised $40M to date. The valuation framework draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series A/B-stage peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors to the $40M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $80M. Peer-median methodology uses a cohort of six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable given the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $68M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $690M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and clinical/regulatory progress, which typically drive biotech valuation dispersion at this stage. The peer-median method pulls materially higher than stage-progression, suggesting either that Epoch's peer set skews toward well-capitalized programs or that comparable funding environments differ from the 2.0x historical step-up assumption. Stage and clinical-stage data sufficiency remain the binding constraints on precision.
